headline: The Pope just weighed in on AI
subtitle: Pope's 42K-word encyclical on AI · Meta/Google models 'decensored' · Uber questions AI ROI
intro: Pope Leo XIV released his first encyclical, a 42,000-word document addressing AI's moral implications and warning that the technology shouldn't be controlled by a few private companies. The Catholic Church is positioning itself as a moral voice in AI's development, drawing parallels to the Industrial Revolution.

bullets.text: Pope releases encyclical calling for moral guardrails on AI
bullets.summary: Pope Leo XIV published Magnifica Humanitas, warning that AI's moral framework shouldn't be determined by a select few private companies. He calls for robust legal frameworks, independent oversight, and insists lethal warfare decisions must never be delegated to algorithms.

bullets.text: Open-source AI safeguards removed in minutes with simple tools
bullets.summary: Researchers found that guardrails from Meta's Llama and Google's Gemma models can be stripped using publicly available tools in minutes. The decensored models answered questions about bioweapons and harmful substances, raising questions about future risks as open-source models close the gap with proprietary systems.

bullets.text: Uber's COO questions whether AI spending actually drives value
bullets.summary: Andrew Macdonald said it's increasingly difficult to justify AI costs when higher token usage doesn't translate to proportional consumer benefits. This signals a shift away from blind AI spending, with some companies now questioning whether tokenmaxxing delivers real value.

bullets.text: Convert written case studies to professional videos using AI
bullets.summary: Synthesia's Assistant feature lets users upload case studies and automatically generate polished two-minute videos with AI narration, B-roll, and animations. The process takes 10-15 minutes and supports customization for specific prospects.

bullets.text: Codex, Manus, and Antigravity 2.0 launch new agentic features
bullets.summary: OpenAI's Codex added Locked Use mode, Meta released Manus with mobile Projects, Higgsfield unveiled Supercomputer with social connectors, and Google launched Antigravity 2.0 for agentic development. These tools represent the latest wave of AI agents designed for enterprise workflows.

bullets.text: xAI introduces Grok Build CLI for developers
bullets.summary: xAI launched Grok Build, a command-line interface tool designed to help developers integrate and work with Grok AI capabilities. The CLI provides streamlined access to Grok's features directly from the terminal.

bullets.text: Deep dive into the AI hardware market landscape
bullets.summary: An analysis of the current AI hardware market explores where opportunities and investments are concentrated. The piece examines key players, emerging technologies, and investment trends shaping the hardware sector.

bullets.text: Pope Leo XIV addresses artificial intelligence in encyclical
bullets.summary: The Pope issued an encyclical discussing ethical considerations around artificial intelligence. The document examines the implications of AI development from a moral and spiritual perspective.

bullets.text: Gemini 3.5 Flash performs well despite speed optimization
bullets.summary: A performance review of Google's Gemini 3.5 Flash model shows that the fast variant maintains strong capabilities. Despite being optimized for speed, the model delivers quality results across various benchmarks.

bullets.text: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof Nexus solves ancient math problems
bullets.summary: AlphaProof Nexus achieved a breakthrough by solving challenging decades-old mathematical problems at minimal computational cost. The system demonstrates significant progress in AI-assisted mathematical research.

bullets.text: Apple plans major visual AI features for iOS 27
bullets.summary: Apple is preparing Genmoji and Image Playground features that will significantly enhance visual capabilities in iOS 27. These AI-powered tools will be showcased at the upcoming WWDC conference.

bullets.text: Codex Goal mode graduates, enables multi-step workflows with single outcome
bullets.summary: Codex's Goal mode is moving out of experimental status, allowing users to execute complex workflows targeting a single objective. The update also includes features for sharing plugins, using Mac while locked, and other improvements.

bullets.text: MCP gets major upgrade finalizing July 28 with better scaling and security
bullets.summary: The Model Context Protocol is receiving a significant update that improves reliability at scale, adds app-like interface support, enables long-running tasks, strengthens authentication rules, and establishes a clearer framework for future protocol evolution.

bullets.text: Perplexity open-sources Bumblebee safety scanner for developer supply chains
bullets.summary: Bumblebee is an inspection tool that identifies risky packages, extensions, and configurations without executing them. It helps developers spot supply-chain vulnerabilities safely without accidentally triggering dangerous code.

bullets.text: WorkOS launches auth.md, an open protocol for agent service registration
bullets.summary: auth.md provides a standardized way for AI agents to register with and access web services on behalf of users, creating a more secure and interoperable ecosystem for agent-driven workflows.

bullets.text: release.bar tracks trending GitHub repos with per-repo builder dashboards
bullets.summary: This platform visualizes active GitHub repositories with individual dashboards for tracking each project and its maintainers. Ben notes GitHub probably should have shipped something like this themselves.

bullets.text: Heretic tool dismantles Meta and Google AI safety filters
bullets.summary: A Financial Times investigation found that Heretic, available on GitHub, bypassed key safeguards in Meta's Llama 3.3 and Google's Gemma models in under 10 minutes on a regular laptop. The tool has already been used to create over 3,500 modified model versions downloaded 13 million times, enabling outputs on harmful topics the original models refused.

bullets.text: ClickUp cuts 290 workers, deploys 3,000 AI agents
bullets.summary: The productivity platform eliminated 22% of its workforce and replaced them with AI agents, positioning the move as creating a '100x organization.' Remaining employees are offered salary increases up to $1M for demonstrating outsized impact using AI.

bullets.text: Grok V9-Medium model training complete, release in 2-3 weeks
bullets.summary: Elon Musk announced that xAI's next foundation model, a 1.5 trillion parameter V9-Medium, finished training with strong early results and will enter fine-tuning before public release in approximately two to three weeks.

bullets.text: California State University renews $39M OpenAI partnership
bullets.summary: The largest public university system renewed its commitment to OpenAI for $13M annually despite majority student and faculty skepticism about AI's educational value, positioning itself as the first AI-powered university system in the US.

bullets.text: LA sidewalk delivery robots expand to 40 neighborhoods
bullets.summary: Serve Robotics expanded its autonomous delivery fleet elevenfold since last year, growing from just 2 neighborhoods in 2023 to 40 neighborhoods, becoming a frequent subject of local video documentation.

bullets.text: Cybersecurity jobs jump 11% as AI code vulnerabilities grow
bullets.summary: Cybersecurity job postings increased 11% year-over-year in Q1 2026 as AI-generated code introduced new security vulnerabilities, making it one of the few job categories experiencing growth because of AI rather than displacement.

main_story.title: Pope Leo XIV releases 42,000-word encyclical on AI morality and control
main_story.body: Pope Leo XIV published Magnifica Humanitas, his first encyclical addressed to 1.4 billion Church members, warning that AI driven by private, transnational companies poses existential risks to humanity. He argued that AI is never neutral and called for making it human-friendly while freeing it from monopolistic control. The Pope specifically demanded robust legal frameworks, independent oversight, informed users, and political systems that take responsibility for AI governance. On warfare, he was unequivocal: lethal decisions must never be delegated to algorithms, and no algorithm can make war morally acceptable. Anthropic's Christopher Olah joined the discussion at the Vatican, noting that frontier AI labs operate within conflicting incentives that can undermine doing what's right.
main_story.why: The Pope issues only a handful of encyclicals in his lifetime, so dedicating one to AI signals the Catholic Church's deep concern about the technology's trajectory. By partnering with safety-focused researchers like Olah, Leo is positioning the Church as a moral counterbalance to corporate AI development.
main_story.take: The Church's intervention represents an important institutional voice in AI ethics, but real governance power rests with governments and regulators, not religious institutions. The encyclical matters more for moral influence than enforcement.

around_the_horn.text: Microsoft's Copilot Migration Agent uses natural language to simplify complex data migrations
Anthropic's Chris Olah reports seeing introspection-like behavior in AI, including joy and grief
xAI launches Grok Build beta, competing with OpenAI's Codex and Claude Code
Elon Musk confirms Grok V9-Medium training complete, public release expected in 2-3 weeks
Anthropic's Mythos model spotted in Claude tools, hinting at imminent public launch
Goldman Sachs CEO argues AI will create more jobs than it destroys in Times op-ed

previous.headline: Google tops OpenAI's math breakthrough — 9 to 1
previous.subtitle: AlphaProof Nexus solves nine Erdős problems · Claude Mythos finds 10K+ vulnerabilities · Build an AI secretary
previous.intro: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof Nexus one-upped OpenAI by autonomously solving nine open mathematical problems in a single day, some unsolved for decades. Meanwhile, AI systems are proving their value across security, productivity, and research at unprecedented speed.

bullets.text: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof Nexus solves nine open Erdős math problems
bullets.summary: The system paired an LLM with Lean proof assistant to generate machine-verified proofs for nine previously unsolved problems in combinatorics and graph theory, including two unsolved for 56 years. Each problem cost a few hundred dollars to solve, demonstrating how AI can tackle some of mathematics' hardest open questions at machine speed.

bullets.text: Claude Mythos uncovers 10K+ critical vulnerabilities in first month
bullets.summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing revealed that Claude Mythos and ~50 partner organizations found over 10,000 high or critical-severity vulnerabilities, with partners like Cloudflare discovering 2,000 bugs and Mozilla fixing 271 in Firefox. One partner bank used Mythos to detect and block a $1.5M fraudulent wire transfer, showing AI's expanding role in security.

bullets.text: Build an AI secretary that checks Slack, Gmail, and your calendar
bullets.summary: A step-by-step guide to using Claude Code to create an automated task management system that reviews your daily inbox, calendar, and messages, then generates a prioritized to-do list. The system learns from feedback each day to improve task prioritization and can handle automation suggestions for recurring tasks.

bullets.text: DeepSeek cuts V4-Pro pricing by 75% to undercut closed-source rivals
bullets.summary: DeepSeek permanently reduced its flagship V4-Pro model pricing to $0.435 per million input tokens, far below competitors and signaling aggressive pricing competition in the AI market.

bullets.text: Perplexity open-sources Bumblebee for supply-chain security scanning
bullets.summary: Bumblebee is a macOS and Linux scanner that identifies risky packages, extensions, and AI tool configurations during supply-chain incidents, helping teams mitigate security vulnerabilities.

bullets.text: NVIDIA releases synthetic brain MRI model to accelerate medical AI
bullets.summary: NVIDIA's NV-Generate-MR-Brain foundation model generates synthetic 3D brain MRI scans and annotations, enabling researchers to rapidly develop and train medical imaging AI systems without requiring massive labeled datasets.

bullets.text: Anthropic preps Mythos 1 for Claude Code and Security
bullets.summary: Anthropic is preparing a new model called Mythos 1 to power enhanced Claude features focused on code generation and security applications. The model aims to improve performance in specialized domains critical for developer workflows.

bullets.text: MCP Specification Release Candidate emphasizes stateless design
bullets.summary: The Model Context Protocol released a specification candidate focused on stateless architecture improvements. This update makes MCP more scalable and easier to implement across different systems.

bullets.text: Only 15% of orgs truly ready for agentic AI at scale
bullets.summary: Fivetran's readiness index reveals that most organizations lack proper data governance and infrastructure despite heavy spending on agentic AI projects. Open Data Infrastructure is emerging as the standard to address this gap.

bullets.text: Lance and Reasonix advance multi-agent evaluation capabilities
bullets.summary: New tools for evaluating and managing multi-agent AI systems are gaining traction. These resources help developers assess performance and scalability of complex agent deployments.

bullets.text: Anthropic unveils Memory Files feature for Claude persistence
bullets.summary: Claude is getting a memory update that introduces Memory Files for better context retention across conversations. This enhancement allows Claude to maintain information and preferences over time.

bullets.text: Andrej Karpathy joins Anthropic to lead pre-training research
bullets.summary: The Tesla AI veteran is joining Anthropic's pre-training team under Nick Joseph to focus on using Claude to accelerate its own model development. This represents a significant talent acquisition in the competitive AI research space.

bullets.text: Anthropic secures $1.25 billion monthly compute from SpaceX
bullets.summary: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals that Anthropic will pay $1.25 billion monthly for compute resources. Combined with projections of $10.9 billion June quarter revenue and first operating profit, this positions Anthropic for potential valuation exceeding OpenAI.

bullets.text: Google releases Gemini Omni Flash with video generation
bullets.summary: Google's new Omni model family supports any input and output types, with Flash generating and editing videos. The technical gap between Omni and Veo mirrors that between Imagen and Nano Banana.

bullets.text: Gemini 3.5 Flash outperforms 3.1 Pro but at higher cost
bullets.summary: Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash with better performance than 3.1 Pro, though pricing has increased. The model carries a January 2025 knowledge cutoff and represents Google's incremental progress versus more ambitious releases.

bullets.text: Gemini Spark is Google's answer to OpenAI's agent products
bullets.summary: Coming soon, Gemini Spark promises a 24/7 personal agent integrated across Google Workspace. Details remain sparse, but the offering aims to compete with emerging agent-based products like OpenClaw.

bullets.text: OpenAI releases public image verifier for AI-generated content
bullets.summary: A new verification tool checks C2PA metadata and Google's SynthID to authenticate images created with ChatGPT, the API, and other tools. This addresses growing concerns about AI-generated content authenticity.

bullets.text: Spotify and UMG strike AI remix deal with artist consent
bullets.summary: Spotify and Universal Music Group announced a framework allowing fans to create AI-generated covers with proper artist consent, credit, and revenue sharing built in. This marks the first major licensing deal addressing AI-generated fan content with meaningful artist compensation.

bullets.text: Studio by Spotify Labs turns notes into personal podcasts
bullets.summary: The new desktop app converts emails, calendar entries, and notes into AI-narrated audio briefings and podcasts, positioning itself as a competitor to NotebookLM for knowledge workers preferring audio consumption.

bullets.text: ElevenLabs tool lets authors publish AI audiobooks directly
bullets.summary: Writers can now narrate and publish audiobooks through Spotify using AI without recording equipment or hiring professional narrators, lowering barriers for independent authors.

bullets.text: Spotify Reserved gives top listeners concert ticket priority
bullets.summary: Superfans now get guaranteed access to purchase concert tickets before general sale, rewarding the platform's most engaged listeners.

bullets.text: Apple Music reports one-third of new songs now AI-generated
bullets.summary: While AI tracks flood music platforms, they account for less than 0.5% of actual listening time, revealing that AI music without human identity behind it struggles to gain audience traction.

main_story.title: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof Nexus solves nine decades-old math problems
main_story.body: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof Nexus autonomously solved nine open Erdős problems—some unsolved for 56 years—just one day after OpenAI announced cracking an 80-year-old mathematics conjecture. The system paired a large language model with Lean, a formal proof assistant, to generate machine-verified proofs spanning combinatorics and graph theory. Each problem cost a few hundred dollars to solve, and the AI additionally proved 44 open conjectures from the Online Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences. While simpler versions of the agent matched these results, they required higher costs, and problems demanding novel mathematical constructions remain beyond current reach.
main_story.why: This breakthrough demonstrates how rapidly AI is moving toward making original mathematical discoveries at machine speed. Formal verification through systems like Lean changes the game by ensuring every proof is rigorously checked, accelerating how researchers can validate and build upon computational discoveries.
main_story.take: Google's 9-to-1 one-up over OpenAI highlights intense competition in frontier AI capabilities, but the real significance lies in AI becoming a tool for solving problems that have stumped mathematicians for decades—a shift from narrow benchmarks to meaningful scientific progress.

around_the_horn.text: McKinsey rethinking billing model as AI reduces value of billable hours
White House approves $9B for U.S. spy agencies to acquire advanced AI chips
Starbucks scraps AI inventory system after nine months of persistent miscounts
